name: Ph.D. in Computer Science

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: As a research doctorate a light course footprint is expected, but the page surfaces only three courses (CS 725/726 Independent Study and the 0-credit CS 791 Graduate Seminar) with requirementGroups=0, so there is no visible structure for breadth/qualifier coursework, candidacy or dissertation research.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the doctoral milestone structure (breadth/qualifier coursework, candidacy exam, dissertation-research registration) so the research progression is legible on the catalog page.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: With only 3 referenced courses, deepestPrereqChain=0 and missingCourseCount=0, there is essentially no prerequisite graph to evaluate; the registration gates that exist (advisor approval for CS 725/726) are administrative rather than academic.
dimensions.suggestion: Point students to the graduate CS course catalog they draw advanced breadth coursework from, so the research page connects to the wider curriculum.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning or research outcomes are stated in the provided data.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish doctoral learning outcomes framed as research competencies (independent research, scholarly communication, dissertation defense).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits=null and creditReconcileDelta=null leave the total credit requirement and dissertation-credit minimums unstated, which is a transparency gap even for a research degree.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total post-bachelor's/master's credit requirement and the minimum dissertation-research credits.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page is sparse: only 3 courses, no requirement groups, no outcomes and no description of admission, qualifiers, candidacy or dissertation timeline, so a prospective student cannot infer the program's shape.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a program-structure section (admission, qualifying/breadth exams, candidacy, dissertation, expected timeline).

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Research currency cannot be judged from three administrative course entries (CS 725/726 independent study, CS 791 seminar); the seminar mechanism supports exposure to current topics but no research areas are surfaced.
dimensions.suggestion: List active research areas and labs (AI/ML, security, systems, theory) so prospective students can see the program's current directions.

descriptionQuality.justification: The three descriptions are present but largely administrative (registration restrictions and advisor-approval boilerplate for CS 725/726; a brief seminar summary for CS 791), so they convey process rather than substantive research content.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are null and the registration conditions (doctoral-student restriction, advisor approval) are stated clearly and consistently with missingCourseCount=0, though there is little prerequisite structure to assess.

missingTopics.topic: Formal research-methods & responsible conduct of research
missingTopics.rationale: Only independent study and a seminar appear; no required research-methods/RCR course is listed for doctoral training.
No breadth requirement across CS areas (theory, systems, AI) is visible, unlike a typical PhD core.
The three courses omit a dissertation-research registration (e.g., a CS 79x dissertation course) central to doctoral progress.
Beyond CS 791, no area-specific research seminars (AI/ML, security, systems) are surfaced to convey the research frontier.
TA training, grant writing and academic-job preparation are standard doctoral elements not represented on the page.

emergingTrends.trend: Generative AI / LLMs and foundation models
emergingTrends.rationale: A dominant CS research thrust the program should surface through advanced seminars and dissertation areas.
Fairness, privacy and safety in ML are fast-growing doctoral research directions worth advertising.
Ties CS doctoral work to the college's data-science and AI programs for cross-area dissertations.

emergingTrends.trend: Reproducibility & open science in CS research
emergingTrends.rationale: Increasingly expected of doctoral training and a natural addition to the graduate seminar (CS 791).
An emerging frontier that would signal currency in the program's research portfolio.

topSuggestions: Publish the doctoral program structure and milestones (breadth/qualifier, candidacy, dissertation) on the catalog page.
State the total credit and minimum dissertation-research credit requirements.
Add and advertise required research-methods and responsible-conduct-of-research training.
List faculty research areas and labs to convey current research directions (AI/ML, security, systems, theory).
